authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(113) — Trabuco Creek (a.k.a. Arroyo Trabuco Creek) (Orange Co.)
sources.quotedText: (113) Trabuco Creek (a.k.a. Arroyo Trabuco Creek) (Orange Co.). Downstream of the Interstate Highway 5 bridge to the confluence with San Juan Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 250576, "Trabuco Creek", Stream, Orange Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":250576,"gaz_name":"Trabuco Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Orange"}; geometry multipoint, first point (Web Mercator -13098507.6537, 3960492.4998) converts to lon -117.665896, lat 33.489754, at the creek's mouth (confluence with San Juan Creek near Descanso Park, San Juan Capistrano) — matching the regulated reach's downstream boundary; ~1.3km from the file's previous coordinate (-117.6628, 33.5013), which was off tolerance and has been corrected to this GNIS point.

parameters.note: Trabuco Creek (Arroyo Trabuco) downstream of the Interstate Highway 5 bridge to its confluence with San Juan Creek, near Mission San Juan Capistrano, is closed to all fishing statewide.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing year-round, downstream of the I-5 bridge to the confluence with San Juan Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: Downstream of the Interstate Highway 5 bridge to the confluence with San Juan Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
